sourcesContent:
import React from 'react'; import { Link } from 'react-router-dom'; const mockPhotos = { food: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1498837167922-ddd27525d352?auto=format&fit=crop&w=1200&q=80', appliance: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1484154218962-a197022b5858?auto=format&fit=crop&w=1200&q=80', campus: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1523050854058-8df90110c9f1?auto=format&fit=crop&w=1200&q=80', }; function Home({ currentUser }) { return (
Campus circular marketplace

Buy smarter. Waste less. Keep useful things in motion.

A cleaner way for students to pass along appliances, dorm essentials, and everyday goods before they become landfill.

Explore marketplace {currentUser ? ( Open your profile ) : ( Create account )}
Student Marketplace
Low waste Mini fridge

Picked up in Harvard Square instead of thrown out at move-out.

School Harvard
Region Greater Boston
Mission Less food waste. Less appliance waste. More reuse.
Why it matters

A marketplace with a nonprofit mindset.

When students move quickly, usable microwaves, fridges, storage bins, cookware, and unopened food often get left behind. This platform reframes resale as waste prevention, access, and campus education.

Food systems

Good food should feed students, not bins.

A large share of move-out waste comes from unopened or lightly used food. The site can frame pantry swaps, shared meal kits, and pickup coordination as climate-aware student behavior.

Appliance reuse

Durable items deserve a second student life.

Small appliances often get abandoned because resale feels harder than disposal. A cleaner marketplace experience changes that behavior by making reuse fast, local, and trusted.

Food waste

Leftover groceries can become shared resources.

End-of-semester transitions often leave students with unopened pantry items, meal supplies, and kitchen basics they cannot carry home. A local exchange culture can keep those resources in use.

Appliance waste

Small appliances should not become disposable.

Mini fridges, lamps, fans, kettles, and microwaves are expensive to replace and costly to discard. Extending their life by even one more year materially reduces waste and student spending.

Educational dashboard

Show students the story behind each listing.

Environmental messaging works better when it is concrete. These mock visuals translate everyday campus behavior into simple impact cues that make reuse feel immediate and measurable.

Mock weekly diversion Items redirected from waste
Food
72%
Appliances
61%
Storage
84%
Mock semester trend Awareness rises when reuse is visible

Mock data: as listing activity increases, awareness campaigns have a more practical place to land.

Educational focus

Teach circular habits at the moment students actually need them.

The most effective sustainability tools are practical. Instead of abstract messaging, this app makes reuse visible through nearby listings, regional pickup, shared trust, and simple checkout flows that encourage second life ownership.

Reuse first Default the student decision toward keeping an item in circulation.
Regional pickup School, region, and neighborhood filters make reuse feel immediate.
Visible impact Every listing can communicate the value of redirecting waste away from landfills.
Mock campaign messaging

Designed to feel aspirational, calm, and credible.

The greener palette signals sustainability and trust. Soft neutrals keep the experience premium. Bright white space reduces friction. Together, those choices make the platform feel less like a classifieds board and more like a thoughtful student service.

Green for action Primary calls to action suggest growth, care, and environmental purpose.
Warm neutrals for trust Soft backgrounds reduce stress and support longer reading in educational sections.
Data with emotion Charts, photos, and clear copy work together to make the mission feel concrete.
Start locally

Built for campus communities that want less waste and lower costs.

Browse available items View mock checkout
); } export default Home;
